perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. Recipient will only be responsible for direct breaches of this Agreement and will not be held accountable for actions by its Representatives. The expense of restraining Representatives will be shared between both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original agreement by removing the Recipient's responsibility for breaches by its Representatives and shifting the cost of restraint. This introduces an in-text contradiction within the scope of responsibility, potentially creating a loophole where the Recipient could claim no liability for a Representative's actions. If the Recipient's Representatives disclose Confidential Information, and the Recipient is not responsible, it will be contradictory to Section (10) Injunctive Relief, as now it will difficult to put injunctive relief to the Recipient for breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (10) Injunctive Relief . It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including an injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by the Recipient of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Recipient's responsibility for breaches by its Representatives, and shifts the cost of restraining Representatives. This contradicts Section (10) Injunctive Relief, because Section (10) says that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives, but now the Recipient is only responsible for direct breaches of this agreement and not the Representatives. Therefore, it will be difficult to put injunctive relief to the Recipient for breach if the Representatives breached, making it a clear and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term “Confidential Information” also shall be deemed to include the portion of all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by Recipient or its Representatives that contain Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Confidential Information” shall be deemed to exclude summaries and analyses prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives, even if they contain Confidential Information. These secondary documents are not subject to the restrictions outlined in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original definition of 'Confidential Information' by excluding summaries and analyses created by the Recipient, even if they contain the Disclosing Party's confidential data. This creates an in-text contradiction, because now it's unclear which documents are protected and which are not. The original agreement was to include all documentation that included confidential information, but now excluding them will create and in-text contradiction to (2) Use of Confidential Information , because the Recipient can use and share documentation that has confidential information, since it not being covered by the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) Use of Confidential Information . Recipient and its Representatives may use the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for any other purpose they deem appropriate. Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. Recipient will only be responsible for direct breaches of this Agreement and will not be held accountable for actions by its Representatives. The expense of restraining Representatives will be shared between both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by explicitly excluding summaries and analyses from the definition of 'Confidential Information,' even if they contain confidential data. This conflicts with section (2) Use of Confidential Information, which states that the Recipient must safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. The change allows the Recipient to use and share documentation containing confidential information if it's in the form of summaries or analyses, thus creating a direct contradiction to the intended protection of Confidential Information as outlined in the original text and section (2).

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, the Recipient and its Representatives are released from all obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the enduring obligation of confidentiality after the return or destruction of information. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about the post-termination responsibilities, impacting the enforceability of confidentiality and obligations, as it implies that Recipient is now not responsible once the confidential information has been returned. This will be contradictory to section (8) Term, as now after the term ends, it will be contradictory that the Recipient will not be under any legal obligation, once the confidential information has been returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (8) Term . The Term of this Agreement commences on the date first set forth above and extends for a period of three (3) years thereafter unless otherwise agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that obligations of confidentiality continue even after the return or destruction of confidential information. The changed text states that *all* obligations, including confidentiality, are released upon the return or destruction of confidential information. This directly contradicts section (8), which defines the term of the agreement as three years. If the recipient returns the confidential information before the three-year term is up, according to the changed text, they are released from all obligations, conflicting with the stated term. This is an ambiguity in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified or discharged in whole or in part except by an agreement in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the formal requirement of a written agreement signed by both parties for any modifications. By allowing modification via email, the agreement introduces uncertainty about the validity of alterations to the original contract terms. This will be contradictory to section (14) Entire Agreement, because since it can be modified by email, it does not represent the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (14) Entire Agreement . This Agreement sets forth the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings between them, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text allows modifications via email correspondence, which directly contradicts the requirement of a signed written agreement. This contradiction impacts section (14) Entire Agreement, as the agreement can now be altered without a formal written agreement signed by both parties, meaning the 'entire agreement' is not necessarily captured in the original signed document. The definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction is met, as it makes the definition of what an agreement is unclear.
